summary: Created - add Krea2 (Flux family) end-to-end per docs/add-model-playbook.md. Phase 1 = Turbo (low tier); non-turbo (baseline) later, same card. User owns workflow authoring.
Picked up. Phase 0 = research Krea2 style LoRAs (how many, how applied, prompt-trigger contract + where the style token sits in the prompt).

Research done: research/style-loras.md. 9 style LoRAs (not 4), trigger phrase APPENDED to prompt with ", " after enhancement. ComfyUI docs + our workflow note both list a STALE dead 4-LoRA set. Corrected card: VAE is qwen_image_vae (reuse vae-qwen-image dep, already on R2), NOT vae-flux-ae; text encoder is qwen3vl_4b (new upload).

Research 2: research/sampler-and-resolution.md. (1) cfg0/mu1.15 = krea-ai first-party, NOT conflicting with Comfy cfg1; graph has NO shift node - open q. (2) sampler: ship er_sde/simple (core, 0 deps); RES4LYF res_2s+bong_tangent = follow-up card (custom_nodes dep + 2-stage shape). (3) ResolutionSelector = core formula node, ~1pct off our FLUX_RATIOS and MISSING 4:5/5:8/5:4/8:5 -> DELETE it, reuse FLUX_RATIOS values on the ModelDef (orientation mode). Open: Turbo native 1024-2048, maybe qualityTiers 1k/2k. (4) CustomCombo uses INDEX->subgraph regex line-lookup, decoupled from loader filename = silent drift (already broken). Proposed seam: app injects lora_name+strength as MPI-219 object form + trigger via PrimitiveStringMultiline(value). VERIFIED comfyController:1113 targets has NO string_a/string_b -> StringConcatenate NOT injectable today. (5) User cut in-graph prompt enhancement.

Wrote docs/krea2.md (model-scoped playbook) + routed it in docs/README.md. CORRECTION: RES4LYF is ALREADY a dep (custom_nodes, installRequirements:true -> baked in Pod image, universal by type MPI-222, node_lock nodes.RES4LYF @419de2d7). res_2s/bong_tangent/deis_3m cost ZERO new deps both engines - only the 2-stage workflow SHAPE is the cost. Local-install finding: 9 style LoRAs at G:/CubricModels/loras/krea-2/style/, ComfyUI lists them SUBFOLDERED+backslash (krea-2\style\krea2_darkbrush.safetensors) - live-confirmed via /object_info. YAML was never broken (stale cached model list; browser refresh fixed). Touches MPI-198 open path-heal risk on non-Windows local engine.

Live workflow reviewed. STYLE SYSTEM SETTLED: 9x MpiLoraModel (Input_style_lora_1..9) always present, chained UNET->1..9->Input_Lora_1..6->ToBasicPipe; each strength_model driven by MpiMath (1.0 if a==N else 0.0) off a single MpiInt Input_Style. Input_Style ALSO feeds MpiPromptList.specific_item (VERIFIED 1-indexed, 0=none, prompt_list.py:139/175) -> trigger phrase -> StringConcatenate.string_b after Input_Positive. So APP INJECTS ONE INT (Input_Style, clamp 0..9). No CustomCombo/subgraph. Trigger is APPENDED, confirmed. AGENT ERRORS CORRECTED: (1) mode:4=bypass/2=mute was never checked -> wrongly flagged 2 live samplers + orphaned ModelSamplingAuraFlow; ClownsharK branch(72,77,78,79)+AuraFlow(71)+ResolutionSelector(49)+enhance(58,60) are all BYPASSED, only KSampler(53)+Output_Image(144) live. (2) Output_Image IS a valid capture title (MPI-182 alias, commandExecutor.js:1253) - playbook s8 -Output only- text is STALE. (3) ClownsharK settings (eta.5/res_2s/bong_tangent/8) are users Chroma-tuned config, NOT a community Krea2 recipe (community = res_2s/beta/6 -> deis_3m/bong_tangent/2@0.2). NEXT: sampler A/B (euler-simple-8 | res_2s-bong_tangent-8 | res_2s-beta-6 | er_sde-simple-8), seed+style=0 fixed, shift held bypassed; THEN shift 1.15 vs 1.73 (check ClownsharK internal shift first, double-apply risk); refiner only if detail deficit. Watch: res_2s is 2nd-order (8 steps=16 NFE) on an 8-step-DISTILLED model - oversampling may soften.

SAMPLER INVESTIGATION CLOSED (live A/B, user-run). SHIP: stage1 euler/beta/6/denoise1.0/eta0.5/cfg1 -> stage2 refiner euler/beta/3/denoise0.35/eta0.5 on the LATENT (no VAE round-trip). Stage2 beats stage1-alone at every setting = refiner earns its cost => workflow is MULTISTAGE (needs _stage2 runtime file + capabilities.multiStage + progressStages bar count COUNTED LIVE; 9 NFE vs 6). NO shift node: comfy/supported_models.py:1828 already sets Krea2 shift=1.15 (Kreas mu); AuraFlow verified inert live. Constraints: first-order sampler ONLY (res_2s distorts at 8 AND 4 steps; deis_3m loses); ~6 steps under beta (beta@8 distorts; optimum is SCHEDULER-dependent, bong peaks at 8); symmetric tapered schedule (beta > bong_tangent > simple; simple gave structural artifacts despite smallest max-gap); eta>0 load-bearing (eta 0 degraded ALL 4 configs incl refiner shadows). Refiner tuning invariant: comfy slices sched(int(steps/denoise))[-(steps+1):] so GAP SIZE is the real knob; too-fine gaps read as noise. WARN: denoise 0.35 starts at sigma .329 > stage1 .275 => partial REGENERATION not pure polish (neon/poster/ring changed) -> refiner cannot be a user on/off toggle, and stage2 MUST get the same conditioning (style trigger) as stage1. RES4LYF still used (for eta) but already a dep, zero new cost. SIX agent theories refuted + recorded in docs/krea2.md as do-not-repropose. Transfer lesson: sampler recipe tuned on non-distilled Chroma does NOT transfer to step-distilled Krea2; the one community recipe lost at BOTH stages.

Handoff 618e771a for a RESEARCH-FIRST next session: Krea2 ControlNet (native control-LoRA exists: facok/comfyui-krea2-controlnet + Patil/Krea-2-depth-controlnet weights), i2i/image-edit, Conditioning-Rebalance multi-reference (nova452 - CAUTION: repo also advertises safety-filter removal), and the unverified Coyote NSFW finetune. Base t2i (style LoRAs + sampler) is SETTLED.

RESEARCH SESSION CLOSED (3 threads). (1) REFERENCE-IMAGE CONDITIONING IS NATIVE - no 3rd-party node needed. Krea2Tokenizer.tokenize_with_weights accepts images= (comfy/text_encoders/krea2.py:28) -> qwen3vl swaps token 151655 <|image_pad|> for the raw image tensor (qwen3vl.py:170-172) -> Qwen3VLVisionModel embeds it (qwen3vl.py:58,65) + DeepStack injection (L113-116) -> rides cross_attn -> krea2/model.py:252 txtfusion fuses it. NOT captioning (no string ever produced => Cubric Prompt CANNOT replicate this; a caption is a lossy bottleneck, vision embeddings are not) and NOT editing (no latent concat, no structural pin) => it is STYLE/SUBJECT REFERENCE. USER HYPOTHESIS ('just reads images and augments the prompt') REFUTED by source. Core nodes TextEncodeQwenImageEdit(+Plus) already pass images= (comfy_extras/nodes_qwen.py:46,102), <=3 images, ZERO new deps. TRAP: those nodes' optional vae input attaches reference_latents, and Krea2.extra_conds (model_base.py:2282) reads ONLY cross_attn -> latents SILENTLY DROPPED. Leave vae unwired. OPEN/UNVERIFIED: those nodes override llama_template with Qwen-Image's *edit* template vs Krea2's own *describe* KREA2_TEMPLATE (krea2.py:20) - needs a live A/B. (2) nova452 Conditioning-Rebalance: SKIP. Krea2EncodeRebalance duplicates the core node; Krea2EditRebalance (the 4-image one) HARDCODES guidance(cond_raw,compiled,-0.5) which IS the advertised safety-filter-defeat path - not a toggle, inseparable. Supply chain itself is clean (~450 lines pure tensor math, zero pip deps, no network/blobs/pickle). Not worth a custom_nodes dep. (3) ControlNet: NATIVE path real, DEPTH ONLY. facok/comfyui-krea2-controlnet = 3 nodes, zero pip deps, uses ModelPatcher (no global patching), ships NO preprocessors (needs comfyui_controlnet_aux). Patil/Krea-2-depth-controlnet DOWNLOADED -> G:/CubricModels/loras/krea-2/control/ (861995928 bytes, matches HF content-length). Header read: 450 tensors, all F32, prefixes blocks+first, first.weight=[6144,128] - the WIDENED input projection (128 cols vs base 64) TENSOR-PROVES the channel-concat mechanism. LoRA keys are .A/.B not lora_A/lora_down. No canny/pose/lineart/normal weights exist publicly. The 'Qwen base -> hand to Krea2' video = MISCHARACTERIZATION of the Qwen-VAE encoding the control image inside a single-pass control LoRA; no two-model handoff exists in any source. (User is separately testing a real Qwen+Krea combo - distinct, untested axis, not refuted.) (4) i2i: nothing official; Comfy-Org/Krea-2 (146GB) is t2i weights only; plain latent img2img (VAEEncode -> denoise<1.0) is the only fully-native path and costs ZERO deps. (5) 'Coyote' NSFW finetune NOT FOUND on HF or Civitai as of today (7 searches). Near-matches exist (uzumix/krea2_nsfw LoRA verified; several Civitai ckpts UNVERIFIED - Civitai geo-blocked). Re-check in days. Wrote research/image-conditioning-and-controlnet.md + brief.md Plan Drift (brief was stale on 3 points: vae-flux-ae, single-stage, bare Output).

WEIGHTS DOWNLOADED to G:/CubricModels (both verified: byte count == HF content-length, safetensors header read). (1) Patil/Krea-2-depth-controlnet -> loras/krea-2/control/depth-control-lora.safetensors, 861995928 B. Header: 450 tensors, all F32, prefixes blocks+first, first.weight=[6144,128] = the WIDENED input projection (128 cols vs base Krea2's 64) which TENSOR-PROVES the channel-concat control mechanism. LoRA keys are .A/.B, NOT lora_A/lora_down (matters for any loader). Subfoldered on purpose (user-confirmed) to mirror loras/krea-2/style/ -> ComfyUI lists it as 'krea-2\control\depth-control-lora.safetensors', so it rides the SAME MPI-229/MPI-198 cross-platform path heal as the 9 style LoRAs. Still needs the facok custom node + a depth preprocessor (comfyui_controlnet_aux) before it runs. (2) Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI -> text_encoders/qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors, 9384670680 B. Header: 1446 tensors, BF16/F32/F8_E4M3, prefixes lm_head+model+scaled_fp8+visual, 714 visual.* tensors, embed_tokens=[152064,3584]. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: this is Qwen2.5-VL-7B (hidden 3584) for the USER'S separate Qwen+Krea2 combo experiment. It is NOT Krea2's encoder and MUST NOT enter dependencies.js for MPI-242. Krea2's encoder is qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled.safetensors (Qwen3-VL-4B, hidden 2560), ALREADY on disk since 2026-07-08 - my earlier assumption that it was an outstanding gap was WRONG (corrected by user). PATH-HEAL SCOPE CLARIFIED: loras/ is the ONLY tree ComfyUI recurses with relative paths, so only LoRAs surface backslashed/subfoldered and only they touch the path heal. text_encoders/ is flat + listed by basename -> zero path-heal exposure. NOTE (not app code, no action taken): events.jsonl line 5 (written 2026-07-09) is unparseable JSON - it embeds a literal Windows path in a prose 'summary' field, and \s is an invalid JSON escape. Untracked by git, read by no app code (grepped js/ routes/ scripts/ main.js: zero hits). Cosmetic; user opted to leave kanban history untouched.

CORRECTION + new plan. (A) MY EARLIER 'skip the 3rd-party pack, use core TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus' WAS WRONG. User cites an expert live comparison: Krea2EditRebalance > TextEncodeKrea2 > TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus, and the metric is PROMPT ADHERENCE not image quality. Adherence is a pure conditioning property (decided before step 1), and source review explains it mechanically: core node (1) OVERRIDES llama_template with Qwen-Image's *edit* template while Krea2 was trained on KREA2_TEMPLATE's *describe* template (krea2.py:20), (2) hard-caps refs at 384x384, (3) accepts a VAE whose reference_latents Krea2 SILENTLY DROPS. Confirmed Krea2 NOT in the reference_latents consumer list; QwenImage IS (grepped model_base.py class bodies: Flux, Lumina2, WAN*, TripoSplat, HiDreamO1, Omnigen2, QwenImage - no Krea2). LESSON: verifying a node CARRIES images != verifying it carries them AS THE MODEL EXPECTS. Mechanically equivalent, semantically wrong. (B) THIRD NODE IDENTIFIED = TextEncodeKrea2 (ethanfel/ComfyUI-Krea2TextEncoder). Source read: forces KREA2_TEMPLATE, megapixel scaling (vision_megapixels default 1.0, max 8.0), NO VAE input. Its docstring INDEPENDENTLY derives the reference_latents finding ('no slot for a reference latent, so a VAE input would be a no-op here and is deliberately omitted'). KEY: describe-vs-edit is a KNOB not a bug - the node ships system_prompt input + both KREA2_SYSTEM_DEFAULT (in-distribution) and KREA2_INSTRUCT_SYSTEM (author-labelled 'out-of-distribution, experimental'). That A/B may be the entire reason Rebalance wins. Also: mask crops the ref to bbox (reference masking, NOT inpainting - Krea2 has no inpaint path). (C) REBALANCE RISK RESTATED: if the guidance(cond_raw,compiled,-0.5) math is WHAT MAKES ADHERENCE BETTER, then quality and the unfilter are ENTANGLED, not separable. That is the adoption question, not supply chain (which is clean). Bench TextEncodeKrea2 first; if it closes the gap, skip the pack. (D) CONTROLNET - user was RIGHT: NO real ControlNet for Krea2. Only the depth control-LoRA. No side-network ControlNet, no union/multi, no canny/pose/lineart/normal weights anywhere (katop1234/krea_controlnet = empty stub). CRITICAL FRAMING: preprocessors (OpenPose/Canny/DepthAnything in comfyui_controlnet_aux) are model-agnostic and ALL exist; the scarce thing is the trained CONTROL ADAPTER. Pose map into the depth LoRA = garbage (its widened proj was trained on depth statistics), not an error. GOOD NEWS: facok's loader is control-type AGNOSTIC (channel_mode/normalize are format flags; type determined by the checkpoint) and Tanmaypatil123/Krea-2-controlnet's recipe trains 'depth, canny, tile, gray, or any custom pixel-aligned control signal'. => wire a control-type SELECTOR, don't hardcode depth. (E) NEW PRIORITY - QWEN->KREA2 SAMPLER HANDOFF (user's image-editor plan): Qwen-Image edits well but quality is mediocre; Krea2 is high-quality+fast. Run early steps on Qwen (edit semantics/layout), late steps on Krea2 (texture/fidelity), hand off the LATENT mid-sampling. STRUCTURALLY SOUND - verified supported_models.py: Krea2 (L1823) and QwenImage (L1851) BOTH latent_format=Wan21 (16ch), BOTH shift=1.15, BOTH qwen_image_vae. => latent is directly consumable, no VAE round-trip. Only conditioning differs (qwen3vl_4b vs qwen25_7b) => TWO Load CLIP + two encode nodes, one per stage. This is why the 7B encoder was downloaded. ASYMMETRY: wire VAE on the Qwen side (consumes reference_latents), NOT the Krea2 side (inert). OPEN: handoff step N / sigma alignment (Krea2-Turbo is 6-step distilled on beta; Qwen is not distilled), whether Krea2 respects Qwen's edit semantics or pulls back toward its own prior (cf. the stage-2 refiner at denoise 0.35 re-entering structure). BENCH ORDER: (1) Qwen->Krea2 handoff, (2) TextEncodeKrea2 vs Rebalance adherence, (3) depth ControlNet. All weights on disk. NO app wiring yet.

QWEN->KREA2 HANDOFF: TESTED LIVE, CLOSED AS A DEAD END (user-run, 896x1152, 'change her dress to a red dress with a skirt'). Qwen-alone edited ONLY the dress. Qwen->Krea2 gave visibly better quality but ALSO changed the wall poster, the pillow, and the phone. NOT a tuning failure - STRUCTURAL, do not re-open by moving the cut point. ROOT: locality lives in reference_latents. QwenImage CONSUMES them (verified: grepped model_base.py class bodies -> Flux, Lumina2, WAN21/HuMo/S2V/SCAIL, TripoSplat, HiDreamO1, Omnigen2, QwenImage). Krea2 DOES NOT. Krea2's sequence is [text_tokens, noisy_image_patches] (comfy/ldm/krea2/model.py docstring) - no reference-latent slot, no spatial conditioning channel, NOWHERE to say 'keep this region'. So the handoff propagates FIDELITY globally but cannot propagate LOCALITY. Krea2's late steps re-decide every pixel above their sigma floor. THIS EXACTLY REPRODUCES the documented stage-2 refiner effect (docs/krea2.md, denoise 0.35 starts at sigma .329 > stage1's .275 => 'neon sign, wall poster, a ring appeared'). Poster/pillow/phone = SAME PHENOMENON, new context. Any sigma high enough for Krea2 to add detail is high enough to re-decide structure. Escape hatch considered+rejected: SetLatentNoiseMask (comfy_extras/nodes_mask.py) would confine the denoise to a mask, but that is masked INPAINTING (user paints the region), a different product from 'edit the dress'. => DECISION (user): ship Qwen-Image as its OWN edit operation; users upscale afterward with a better model. An upscale is the SAME global-fidelity pass the handoff was reaching for, but runs AFTER the edit is final so it cannot un-edit anything. Cubric already has upscalers. Correct order, zero new architecture. QWEN'S TWO FLAWS, separated: (1) 'zoom in/out' is LIKELY A FIXABLE BUG not inherent - TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus normalizes the ref to ~1MP (total=1024*1024; scale_by=sqrt(total/(w*h))) before VAE-encoding; if the OUTPUT latent's dims/aspect differ from the ref's post-scale size, Qwen reconstructs at a different scale => reads as zoom. User's 896x1152 = 1.03MP, right at the boundary. TEST: match output latent to ref post-scale dims. (2) mediocre quality is REAL + structural; recovered downstream by an upscale. PRODUCT SHAPE THAT FELL OUT: Qwen-Image = image EDITOR (spatially targeted, reads reference_latents => knows source pixels). Krea2 = REFERENCE-CONDITIONED GENERATION (reads vision EMBEDDINGS only => no pixel memory => recomposes). TWO DIFFERENT OPERATIONS, not two implementations of one. A Krea2 'image edit' op is really a reference/style op - name it accordingly or users will expect locality it cannot deliver. The Rebalance repo calling itself 'image edit' was always the overclaim; this test is the proof. NEXT (user): test Krea2EditRebalance on KREA2 ALONE. Bench order now: (1) Rebalance vs TextEncodeKrea2 adherence on Krea2 alone, (2) ship Qwen-Image edit op, (3) depth ControlNet. DOCS: promoted the durable finding to docs/krea2.md (new section 'Krea2 cannot edit images. It re-composes them.' + a dead-theories row). WARNING: docs/krea2.md is now 385 lines, OVER the repo's 200-line-per-doc rule (was already 333 before this session, so it arrived over budget). Not on the docs/README.md exemption list. Needs a split or an exemption - NOT done, user's call.

RETRACTION + REOPEN. (A) I WAS WRONG TWICE about the Qwen+Krea2 combo, in the same direction. Earlier I called the 'Qwen base -> hand to Krea' video an 'obsolete mischaracterization; do not chase it'. That answered the WRONG QUESTION - it correctly showed the depth control-LoRA doesn't work by handing latents between models, then wrongly generalized to 'no Qwen->Krea2 pipeline exists'. USER CORRECTION: the tutorial was doing CONTROLNET, NOT editing, because QWEN SUPPORTS PREPROCESSING. LESSON: 'mechanism X is not how feature Y works' != 'pipeline X is not worth building'. (B) WHY CONTROLNET SURVIVES THE HANDOFF BUT EDITING DOESN'T - the general principle: EDITING needs LOCALITY to cross the boundary (it lived in reference_latents, which Krea2 cannot read => poster/pillow/phone changed). CONTROLNET needs only STRUCTURE, which is baked into the latent by the time Krea2 receives it. A depth map / pose skeleton constrains the WHOLE FRAME's geometry, so Krea2's late steps re-deciding texture everywhere is the FEATURE, not the bug. The exact failure mode that killed editing is what you want for control. (C) VERIFIED: QWEN-IMAGE HAS A REAL SIDE-NETWORK CONTROLNET, Krea2 does not. comfy/ldm/qwen_image/controlnet.py; loaders load_controlnet_qwen_instantx (comfy/controlnet.py:664) + load_controlnet_qwen_fun (:681); classes QwenImageControlNetModel, QwenImageFunControlNetModel, QwenFunControlNet. Qwen also consumes ref_latents AND reference_latents_method (read QwenImage.extra_conds in full). Krea2 has ONE depth control-LoRA and no reference_latents. COMPLEMENTARY: Qwen = every control type + real ControlNet, but ~1MP + mediocre quality. Krea2 = 2K native + excellent quality, but depth-only control. Qwen's ~1MP ceiling (the root of the zoom artifact) is escaped precisely by letting Krea2 run the late steps at 2K. => NEW PROMISING PIPELINE: Qwen+ControlNet (early, structure) -> Krea2 (late, 2K fidelity). Same latent compat as the editor handoff (Wan21/16ch, shift 1.15, shared qwen_image_vae). No locality to lose. (D) MASKING CORRECTION: I dismissed SetLatentNoiseMask as 'a different product'. WRONG - user already HAS a working masked-edit workflow and Qwen masking IS the planned edit op. Masked edit also kills the zoom artifact (unmasked latent never resampled/rescaled). Masking is not a workaround for Krea2's missing locality; it is how the QWEN editor ships, sidestepping the handoff for editing entirely. (E) DOC STRUCTURE: user asked why krea2 isn't split like LTX-2.3. It should be. LTX pattern = hub + thin index + one file per settled topic: docs/builder/README.md (42L) + docs/builder/research/README.md (37L) + ltx-2.3-tiers.md / lora-strength-law.md / prompt-contract.md / wan-2.2-two-stage-sigmas.md etc. docs/krea2.md is now 385L, over the 200L rule, and is NOT exempt (exemptions = append-only evidence logs or coherent single-subject contracts; krea2.md is 4 subjects: samplers, style LoRAs, resolution, conditioning/control/editing). PROPOSED SPLIT (NOT DONE, needs user approval since it touches docs/README.md routing): docs/krea2/README.md (hub ~50) + samplers.md (~110) + style-loras.md (~60) + resolution.md (~35) + conditioning-and-control.md (~110). (F) USER IS INSTALLING Conditioning-Rebalance now; will test it on KREA2 ALONE first (isolating the variable) before deciding, and suspects Rebalance on the Krea2 side may also rescue the handoff. NO app wiring yet.

(A) CONDITIONING-REBALANCE DROPPED - user tested it: 'just false advertising, it's not really an image editor'. SOURCE PREDICTED THIS EXACTLY: no reference_latents, no VAE encode, never produces a latent; refs enter as vision EMBEDDINGS only => it RECOMPOSES from a reference, cannot preserve pixels. The 'unfilter the model' advertising in the same README was the tell. BONUS: the open question of whether Rebalance's adherence was ENTANGLED with its guidance(cond_raw,compiled,-0.5) unfilter path is now MOOT - never has to be answered. Supply chain was never the problem (~450 lines, zero deps, no network); the CAPABILITY CLAIM was. (B) KREA2 DEPTH CONTROLNET: LIVE-VERIFIED WORKING (user screenshot: source photo -> depth preprocessor (512x623 depth map) -> Krea2ControlImageEncode -> Krea2ControlApply -> sampler; output holds the source pose while Krea2 fully repaints the subject). Cloned facok/comfyui-krea2-controlnet to G:/ComfyUi/ComfyUI/custom_nodes/ @ 79ebfd3 ('Support quantized Krea2 LoRA shape matching' - REQUIRED, our transformer is krea2_turbo_fp8_scaled). comfyui_controlnet_aux was already installed. (C) TWO THINGS PROVEN THAT WERE ONLY REASONED BEFORE: (1) quantized fp8_scaled transformer + rank-64 control LoRA w/ widened first.weight[6144,128] projection loads cleanly (that IS what 79ebfd3 fixes). (2) THE SUBFOLDERED BACKSLASH PATH RESOLVES ON WINDOWS-LOCAL. Queried live /object_info/Krea2ControlLoRALoader -> ComfyUI lists it as 'krea-2\control\depth-control-lora.safetensors'. THAT EXACT STRING is what the app must inject into lora_name. Same shape as the 9 style LoRAs => rides the existing MPI-229 heal. NOTE: non-Windows LOCAL engine still UNVERIFIED (MPI-198 stays open; RunPod does not test it - feedback_runpod_not_local_engine_proof). (D) DEP SURFACE AUDITED BEFORE INSTALL (user asked 'see if that torch is not going to torch us'): NO requirements.txt/pyproject.toml/setup.py/install.py (all 404). Whole repo = 5 files. Every import is stdlib, torch, or a comfy internal (folder_paths, comfy.ldm.common_dit, comfy.model_management, comfy.patcher_extension, comfy.utils, comfy.weight_adapter.lora.LoRAAdapter - ALL verified present in the local install). No subprocess/eval/exec/pickle/network/file-writes in 709 lines. CANNOT touch the torch install. => dependencies.js: type:'custom_nodes', installRequirements:FALSE, pin @79ebfd3 in dev_configs/node_lock.json. (E) WIRING CONTRACT (read from nodes.py, not README): Krea2ControlLoRALoader(model,lora_name,strength) -> MODEL; Krea2ControlApply(model,control_latent) -> MODEL and it RAISES if the MODEL didn't come from the Loader (checks wrapper attachment) - order enforced in code, cannot mis-wire silently. Krea2ControlImageEncode(control_image,vae,resize,...,optional latent) -> (control_latent, encoded_control_image). TRAP: resize='match_latent_size' is the DEFAULT and REQUIRES the 'optional' latent input (raises otherwise) - wire the same EmptyLatentImage that feeds the sampler; it derives target size from latent.shape * vae.spacial_compression_encode(). DEPTH SETTINGS: channel_mode=grayscale, normalize=per_image_minmax, invert=false (generic defaults rgb/none are WRONG for depth). (F) AGENT PROCESS NOTE: I reported comfy/weight_adapter/lora.py as MISSING - that was MY bug, not the install. Passed a POSIX '/g/...' path into Python, which on Windows treats it as literal, not the G: drive. Bash tool translates those; Python does NOT. Use r'G:/...' in python -c. File exists, LoRAAdapter at lora.py:127. BENCH ORDER NOW: (1) Qwen+ControlNet -> Krea2 handoff [the promising one], (2) ship Qwen-Image edit op WITH masking, (3) wire Krea2 depth control into the app w/ a control-type selector, (4) TextEncodeKrea2 vs core, only if a style/subject-reference op is wanted. NO app wiring yet.

(A) Qwen+ControlNet->Krea2 handoff: DROPPED by user ('forget it, depth is enough'). Krea2 depth control is live-verified and sufficient. Do not re-propose the Qwen control handoff. (B) REBALANCE CANNOT HELP i2i EITHER - user wondered if that's where it'd shine (after removing it). It would NOT. Plain i2i feeds the source in as a LATENT on the sampler's latent_image input. Rebalance only ever touched the CONDITIONING tensor (vision embeddings + guidance math); it never produced or read a latent. It would describe the reference into conditioning while the real image content flows through a channel it never touches. SAME failure as the edit test, new label. DO NOT REINSTALL for i2i. (C) i2i IS FREE ON KREA2: VAEEncode(source) -> denoise<1.0. Zero new nodes, zero deps. Verified VAEEncode is model-agnostic ({'samples': vae.encode(pixels)}) and Krea2 is ModelType.FLUX so denoise<1.0 behaves normally. (D) *** NEW CONSTRAINT: THE REFINER PUTS A HARD DENOISE FLOOR (~0.40) ON i2i. *** Stage 2 re-enters at a FIXED sigma (.329 live-measured) because its denoise 0.35 is independent of whatever stage 1 did. If stage 1's ENTRY sigma drops below that, the refiner RE-NOISES the image back ABOVE where stage 1 left it and re-decides the structure the low-denoise i2i was preserving. Computed from beta_scheduler + flow-matching sigmas (shift 1.15), stage1=6 steps: denoise 1.00->entry 1.000 ok; 0.60->0.670 ok; 0.50->0.536 ok; 0.45->0.482 ok; 0.40->0.396 marginal; 0.35->0.331 UNDOES; 0.30->0.261 UNDOES; 0.20->0.140 UNDOES. => GENTLE i2i (denoise 0.2-0.3), exactly the range users want for 'keep most of the source', is INCOMPATIBLE with the two-stage workflow as configured. TWO ESCAPES: (1) hold stage-1 denoise >= 0.45 (fine for strong restyles, useless for subtle), or (2) BYPASS STAGE 2 FOR i2i - below ~0.40 the refiner is not refining, it is regenerating. NOTE the doc says 'the refiner cannot be a user-facing on/off toggle' - that is true for T2I; for I2I it MUST be, because below the floor it is actively harmful. This is the same mechanism as the poster/pillow/phone effect, arriving from a new direction. Recorded in docs/krea2.md + research file. (E) METHOD NOTE: system python has no numpy; ComfyUI's interpreter is G:/ComfyUi/python_embeded/python.exe - use that for any comfy-dependent computation. Sigma figures used a 1000-step reconstruction of model_sampling.sigmas (puts stage2 entry at .362 vs live-measured .329); the ORDERING and therefore the floor are unchanged, but trust .329 as the measured value. USER IS NOW TESTING i2i. No app wiring yet.

*** RETRACTION: MY PREDICTED i2i 'DENOISE FLOOR ~0.40' DOES NOT EXIST. REFUTED LIVE. *** PREDICTION (wrong): stage2 re-enters at a fixed sigma (.329 live) since its denoise 0.35 is independent of stage1; so a stage1 entry sigma below that should make the refiner re-noise ABOVE where stage1 left off and destroy composition. Computed entries (beta_scheduler + flow-matching sigmas, shift 1.15, stage1=6 steps): 0.50->.536, 0.45->.482, 0.40->.396, 0.35->.331, 0.20->.140. Predicted everything <=0.35 wrecked. REFUTATION (user, live): i2i works down to denoise 0.19 (entry sigma ~.13, FAR below stage2's entry) WITH BOTH STAGES RUNNING. Improves composition and light. 'Quite sensitive.' No wrecked structure. User confirmed explicitly that stage 2 was NOT bypassed. ROOT OF MY ERROR (partially traced, ULTIMATELY UNVERIFIED): I assumed stage 2 behaves like core common_ksampler, which adds noise scaled to sigmas[0] (nodes.py:1563 comfy.sample.prepare_noise). BUT THE WORKFLOW'S STAGE 2 IS RES4LYF ClownsharKSampler_Beta, NOT CORE KSampler. RES4LYF has its own noise machinery I never modeled: beta/samplers.py:75 noise_sampler_init(sigma=sigma_max * noise_stdev) - a noise_stdev knob scaling init noise; beta/samplers.py:342-345 'sigmas *= denoise' when a schedule is supplied else get_sigmas(model,scheduler,steps,abs(denoise)), then 'sigmas *= denoise_alt'; beta/rk_sampler_beta.py:335 prepare_sigmas(sigmas, sigmas_override, d_noise, d_noise_start_step, sampler_mode) where d_noise rescales the whole schedule; and sampler_mode (standard/resample/unsample) changes noise+latent handling entirely. I did NOT finish tracing which of these lets low-denoise i2i survive the refiner, and I am NOT going to invent a mechanism. RULE FOR FUTURE SESSIONS: DO NOT re-derive an i2i denoise floor from core-KSampler semantics. Stage 2 is not core KSampler. If a real answer is ever needed, INSTRUMENT THE LIVE RUN (log sigmas + the init-noise tensor norm entering stage 2); do not reason from nodes.py. PRACTICAL UPSHOT: none. i2i ships as-is - both stages, denoise usable down to ~0.19, zero new nodes, zero new deps (VAEEncode is model-agnostic; Krea2 is ModelType.FLUX so denoise<1.0 is normal). Wrong claim STRIPPED from docs/krea2.md and the research file; replaced with the live-verified result + an explicit do-not-re-derive warning. PROCESS LESSON (again, cf. feedback_test_user_instinct_first): I reasoned from the WRONG SOURCE FILE. The workflow uses a custom sampler; I modeled core. A single question to the user ('was the refiner running?') caught it before it shipped into the docs as fact. ASK, or instrument - don't extrapolate a sampler's behavior from a different sampler's source. ALSO: Qwen+ControlNet->Krea2 handoff DROPPED by user ('forget it, depth is enough'). User is now implementing i2i properly.

(A) 'Stylization' SLIDER SHIPPED by user (name chosen from my suggestions; rejected 'style strength' = implies degree when it changes KIND, and 'style variation' = implies randomness when it is monotonic + reproducible). It drives strength_model on the style LoRA. Default 1.0. Live-tested 0.1/0.3/0.5/0.8/1.0 - EACH PRODUCES A DIFFERENT IMAGE, not just 'more style' (0.1 = colour photo w/ ink texture; 1.0 = near-monochrome ink drawing, palette collapses, face restyles to illustration). WORKS AT 0 because the trigger phrase stays in the prompt regardless of LoRA strength - the base model then interprets it. OPEN (untested as deliberate design): should the trigger scale out with the slider? Today prompt says 'monochrome ink wash style' at full force while the LoRA barely applies it. That tension may BE the good part. (B) *** NAG DOES NOT WORK ON KREA2 - CONFIRMED FROM SOURCE. *** User asked if a negative prompt can be added to the distilled Turbo. Core ComfyUI DOES ship it (comfy_extras/nodes_nag.py, NAGuidance, described verbatim as 'enabling negative prompts on distilled/schnell models') and it IS designed to survive cfg 1.0 - it calls disable_model_cfg1_optimization(), flipping the flag at samplers.py:609 that would otherwise skip the uncond pass. BUT it hooks via set_model_attn1_output_patch, and comfy/ldm/krea2/model.py NEVER READS transformer_options['patches'] - grepped every use (L75,88,121,122,140,144,148,161,163,224,228,229,231,252,270): transformer_options is threaded all the way to optimized_attention_masked, but there is NO patch dispatch anywhere. Compare flux/layers.py:199 which does transformer_patches = transformer_options.get('patches', {}). => NAGuidance on Krea2 = SILENT NO-OP that still forces the uncond pass => DOUBLES NFE (9 -> 18) for ZERO effect. Worst failure mode: slower, identical output, no error. KJNodes' NAG is WanVideoNAG (monkey-patches Wan attention classes by name) - also useless. FIX = ~5-line upstream patch to krea2/model.py mirroring flux. Do NOT fork locally; file it upstream.

RATIOS SETTLED + 2K TIER APPROVED. (1) DIMENSIONS MUST BE /16 NOT /8: VAE spacial_compression=8 (comfy/sd.py:724) x DiT patch=2 (comfy/ldm/krea2/model.py:183). Off-multiple does NOT crash - model.py:239 pad_to_patch_size(circular) pads one latent row/col (wrapping content from the opposite edge) then crops. Works, one edge subtly wrong. ALL 9 FLUX_RATIOS values verified /16-clean, and SDXL_RATIOS too (it is the `default:` fallback, so a misconfigured ModelDef still GENERATES - silently with SDXL presets, not a crash. Easy to miss.). (2) 2K TIER PROVEN LIVE: 1024x2048 (2.00MP) = 61.04s vs 896x1152 (0.98MP) = 28.36s. 2.03x pixels -> 2.18x time => TIME SCALES LINEARLY WITH PIXELS, NOT QUADRATICALLY. My '>=4x attention cost' warning was WRONG (bandwidth/MLP-bound at this scale, not attention-bound). Chroma = 58-62s at ~1MP => Krea2 does 2x the pixels in the same wall clock at better quality. User: 'the user knows that when he goes to high resolutions he's going to pay the price anyway'. NOTE the user's 1024x2048 test is 1:2 (0.5000), NOT 9:16 (0.5625) - it proved the 2MP BAND, it does not pin a ratio. Also FLUX's own 9:16 entry (768x1344) is really 0.5714 - the table's labels are already approximate. (3) SHAPE: declare BOTH `ratios` and qualityTiers:['1k','2k'] on the ModelDef. Declaring qualityTiers auto-flips RATIO_MODES['krea2'] to 'quality' (ratios.js:222). Each tier MUST be a FLAT 9-ENTRY ARRAY carrying both orientations' labels (1:1 once - square is orientation-free), exactly like LTX/Wan - because getModelRatios indexes a declared table by ONE key (ratios.js:276) and _buildQualityOptions (MpiOptionSelector.js:167) does .find(r => r.label === selectedRatio) across tiers. Verified: labels unique per tier; switching 1k<->2k preserves the selected label. (4) CHROMA NEEDS NO GATING (user asked): `declared` returns EARLY at ratios.js:274, so Krea2 never reaches the switch and Chroma's `case 'chroma'` never sees a declared table. The 2K tier lives on Krea2's ModelDef, NOT inside FLUX_RATIOS, so FLUX_RATIOS is never mutated. ZERO changes to ratios.js / Chroma / MpiOptionSelector. (5) ONE GAP: QUALITY_LABELS (MpiOptionSelector.js:141) has '2k' but NOT '1k' -> add "'1k': '1K'," or the tier picker renders undefined. (6) Krea2 will be the FIRST model to use the MPI-174 declared-ratios path - grepped: NO model in models.js sets `ratios:` today, so DECLARED_RATIOS_BY_TYPE is empty and that branch has NEVER executed in production. Expect to find its bugs. TABLES: 1k = 1:1 1024x1024 | 3:4 896x1152 | 4:5 896x1088 | 5:8 768x1280 | 9:16 768x1344 | 4:3 1152x896 | 5:4 1088x896 | 8:5 1280x768 | 16:9 1344x768. 2k = 1:1 1472x1472 | 3:4 1248x1664 | 4:5 1280x1600 | 5:8 1120x1792 | 9:16 1088x1936 | 4:3 1664x1248 | 5:4 1600x1280 | 8:5 1792x1120 | 16:9 1936x1088. All /16-clean, 1.91-2.07MP; 1472x1472 matches LTX's 2K square for cross-model consistency.

(4) VRAM TABLE IS COMPUTED, NOT AUTHORED (footprint.js, MPI-168): tradeTable() derives from weight sizes + 3 fitted constants (OVERHEAD=1.3, K=0.25, MIN_FLOOR=8). Krea2 core weights = 17.36GB (transformer 13.14 + qwen3vl_4b 4.88 + vae 0.24). vramFloor = max(8, 0.25*22.1) = 8GB (MIN_FLOOR clamps). Computed curve: 8GB VRAM=>16GB RAM, 16GB=>8GB RAM, 24GB=>0 (fully resident). So the formula ALREADY independently lands on the 8GB the users report. (5) MpiLoraModel SHORT-CIRCUITS AT STRENGTH 0 (loras.py:100 `if strength_model == 0 or lora_name == None: return (model,...)` - no load_lora_cached call), and the MpiMath gate zeroes 8 of 9 => only ONE style LoRA is ever resident at runtime (0.44GB, not 3.94GB). But totalWeightsGb() sums EVERY resolved dep => it would over-count by 3.50GB. Curve is unchanged at the 8/16/24 rows (floor is MIN_FLOOR-clamped either way), so this is cosmetic today - but note it if a future model ships many mutually-exclusive LoRAs. NOTE: no model has EVER declared a LoRA as a dep (grep type:lora = 0 hits); Krea2s 9 style LoRAs ship WITH the model so they must become deps.

CORRECTION (user caught me): my earlier claim `no model has EVER declared a LoRA as a dep` is FALSE. I grepped for `type: lora`, but WEIGHT deps carry NO `type` field at all - only custom_nodes and json do. Four LoRA deps ship today: LTX-2.3 has THREE (ltx23-lora-merged 3.87GB, ltx23-lora-transition 372MB, ltx23-lora-talkvid 1.1GB) and wan22-5b has one (wan22-5b-turbo-lora 317MB). dependencies.js:386 says it explicitly: `Baked LoRAs - loaded by the workflow (not user slots), travel with the model`, and models.js calls LTX `First model with non-merged baked LoRAs ... shipped as deps, NOT user slots`. => Krea2 style LoRAs as deps is a PROVEN pattern, not a first. Declare them like LTX: filename `loras/krea-2/style/*.safetensors` (subfoldered, R2-mirrored), no `type` field, `size` string. STILL TRUE + now sharper: isWeightDep() = (dep.size && type!==custom_nodes && type!==json), so EVERY LoRA dep counts toward totalWeightsGb(). For LTX that is CORRECT (all 3 baked LoRAs load on every run; totalWeights=61.40GB, floor 15.35, rows 16=>48 ... 64=>0 - matches the shipped table). For Krea2 it OVER-COUNTS by 3.50GB, because the 9 style LoRAs are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: MpiLoraModel.apply_lora short-circuits at strength_model==0 (loras.py:100, returns before load_lora_cached) and the MpiMath gate `b if a==N else 0.0` zeroes 8 of 9 => only ONE style LoRA is ever resident. MEASURED IMPACT: NONE. declared-deps 22.10GB / runtime-worst 18.60GB / bare 17.36GB ALL produce the identical table (floor pinned by MIN_FLOOR=8; ceil(spill/8)*8 absorbs the delta): 8GB VRAM=>16GB RAM, 16=>8, 24=>0 resident. So ship Krea2 with all 10 LoRAs as normal deps and DO NOT special-case footprint.js. Add a code comment so nobody `fixes` the over-count later. Only revisit if a future model ships many mutually-exclusive LoRAs whose sum crosses a floor/row boundary.

FALSE since MPI-182: commandExecutor.js:1253 sets _imageOutputTitle=`output_image` as the TIER-2 alias, matching is case-insensitive, and 7 shipping workflows use Output_Image/Output_image (Chroma t2i/upscaler/detailer, NVIDIA PiD, krea2 t2i). That stale line would make a future agent BREAK a working tier-2 workflow. CORRECTED with the tier table (tier-1 bare `Output` = SDXL/Pony/Illustrious 18 workflows; tier-2 `Output_Image` = Chroma/PiD/Krea2 7 workflows), per user warning `that is two different tiers, careful`. Tier is models.js @property [tier]: 1=legacy bare titles, 2=Input_/Output_ prefixed. NOT interchangeable, never normalize one to the other. ALSO SHIPPED: new project skill .claude/skills/mpi-add-model/SKILL.md (user-invocable /mpi-add-model) whose STEP 0 is a MANDATORY full read of the playbook before any other tool call, plus a step-1 `read the workflow JSON, do not trust prior notes` (mode 4=bypass/2=mute; saved json lags the canvas), a pre-flight trap table pointing at playbook sections, hard rules (never hand-edit workflow JSON; R2 upload+delete need user approval), and a DoD that requires writing anything newly learned BACK into the playbook. CLAUDE.md updated: the Add-a-New-Model router now says RUN THE SKILL first, keeps MUST-READ on the playbook, and states explicitly that a handoff / model-scoped doc ASSUMES the playbook and does not replace it. Skills table row added. Playbook is exempt from the 200-line rule (docs/README.md:21), now 570 lines.

VRAM RESEARCH CLOSED (docs/krea2/resolution.md). USER ASKED: users claim Krea2 runs on 8GB VRAM - is it real? ANSWER: conditional and MIS-LABELED in most cases. NO first-party hardware spec exists anywhere (krea-ai GitHub, HF model card, ComfyUI docs tutorial, krea.ai technical report all publish ZERO VRAM requirements - anyone citing an official 8GB figure is citing nothing). The 8GB claims resolve to THREE different things, only ONE of which is our path: (1) community GGUF Q4 ~7.2-7.7GiB, fits fully resident, MOST LIKELY REFERENT, needs a community-forked loader (city96 throws `Unexpected architecture type`) - WE DO NOT SHIP GGUF (MPI-190 removed it), so this path does not exist in Cubric Vision; (2) fp8_scaled + ComfyUI DynamicVRAM RAM-spilling = WHAT WE SHIP, two confirmed reports (RTX A2000, RTX 3060 Ti), works but needs ~24GB system RAM and est ~31s/img on a 3060 Ti; (3) official NVFP4 7.67GiB, fits 8GB but is BLACKWELL-ONLY (RTX 50xx hardware format). COMFORTABLE PRACTICAL MINIMUM = 16GB VRAM (RTX 5080 confirmed, ~3.5GB headroom). CRITICAL RECONCILIATION - our computed table is CORRECT, no tweak needed: footprint.js yields floor 8GB (MIN_FLOOR clamp, since 0.25*22.10=5.5<8) with rows 8=>16GB RAM, 16=>8, 24=>0 resident. Research measured PEAK SAMPLING VRAM = 12.40GB; our transformer alone is 12.24GB. They MATCH because ComfyUI EVICTS the Qwen3-VL-4B text encoder (4.88GB) BEFORE the sampler loads - encoder and transformer are NEVER both resident. So the text encoder is NOT an OOM culprit (zero reports attribute an OOM to it). And the apparent gap between our 16GB RAM row and the researchs ~24GB is NOT a contradiction: footprint.js states MODEL need, not SYSTEM total (its header: OS reserve ~10-20GB is the users own headroom, surfaced as a footnote, never baked in). 16GB model + OS reserve ~= 24GB observed. CONSISTENT. => DO NOT special-case footprint.js for Krea2; the formula independently lands on the same 8GB floor the users report. SPEED REFERENCE (RTX 3090, 1024^2, 8 steps): fp8_scaled = 14.82 s/img; int8_convrot = 7.70 s/img (1.92x faster via Ampere tensor cores) - a future quant lever. NO 4090 timing exists anywhere. AMD ROCm is BROKEN (kernel crash at step 1) - relevant to the portable Linux build. UI COPY GUIDANCE: quote 16GB as the comfortable minimum; 8GB is the honest floor with a real RAM cost.

int8 PRESERVED as a proper research file: docs/builder/research/krea2-int8-quant.md (100 lines), routed in docs/builder/research/README.md. It was only a one-line speed footnote in docs/krea2/resolution.md - wrong home for a future perf lever. FINDING: krea2_turbo_int8_convrot = 7.70 s/img vs fp8_scaled 14.82 s/img on an RTX 3090 @1024^2/8 steps => 1.92x, via Ampere INT8 IMMA tensor cores (convrot = convolution-rotation weight reordering). CRITICAL DE-CONFLICTION: quant-and-coldstart-investigation.md:172 says `INT8 mixed (Winnougan ~29GB) - Ampere-targeted, no 5090 benefit, barely smaller. Skip.` That verdict is CORRECT AND STILL STANDS - it is scoped to LTX on BLACKWELL (RTX 5090), which has native fp8/mxfp8 tensor cores so fp8 already saturates the pipeline and int8 buys nothing. Krea2s evidence is AMPERE (3090), which has NO native fp8, so INT8 IMMA is the faster path. SAME FACT, OPPOSITE CONCLUSION, DIFFERENT SILICON. `Ampere-targeted` was the REASON TO REJECT it there and is the REASON TO WANT it here. I added a scoping warning box directly under that LTX skip line so no future agent cites it to dismiss a Krea2/Ampere int8 proposal (or cites the new doc to reopen LTX-on-Blackwell int8). IMPLEMENTATION SEAM ALREADY EXISTS + IS PROVEN: this is the MPI-200 runtime-VARIANT axis. gpuArch.js returns blackwell|modern|legacy (modern = Ada/Ampere/Turing). models.js ltx-23 already declares variants.arch.options.{blackwell:{extraDeps:[ltx23-transformer-mxfp8],workflowSuffix:_mxfp8}, modern:{extraDeps:[ltx23-transformer-fp8],workflowSuffix:_fp8}} and resolveModelDeps installs ONLY the matching weight; archVariantOptions() drives the MPI-209 model-manager toggle. A Krea2 int8 variant is a near-copy MINUS the workflowSuffix (graph unchanged, only the UNETLoader weight differs). SIX GATES BEFORE ADOPTION (all open): (1) confirm krea2_turbo_int8_convrot.safetensors exists + source + hash/size; (2) does ComfyUI 0.27 UNETLoader load it NATIVELY (0.27 IS the int8 release) or does it need a community fork - a fork is likely disqualifying, per the GGUF/MPI-190 lesson; (3) REPRODUCE THE 1.92x ON OUR 4060 Ti (Ada, `modern`) - the bench is Ampere, Adas INT8 path differs, DO NOT ASSUME IT TRANSFERS; (4) seeded face A/B at 1K+2K (fp8 was rejected on LTX for eyes/teeth; int8 is a DIFFERENT strategy - integer + per-block scale, not reduced-precision float - so that verdict neither predicts nor exonerates it); (5) Blackwell behaviour - if neutral-or-worse on a 5090 the variant is modern-only and Blackwell keeps fp8, which variants.arch expresses natively; (6) storage cost - a 2nd ~12GB transformer = 2nd R2 upload + 2nd per-user download; archVariantOptions makes it an install TOGGLE not a forced download. DO NOT: ship int8 as default on one third-party 3090 bench; add a GGUF path (MPI-190, do not re-raise); conflate int8_convrot with the Winnougan INT8-mixed weight the LTX doc rejected (different weight, model, silicon target). If adopted => NEW CARD, not MPI-242. Krea2 ships fp8_scaled first. Also captured in that doc: AMD ROCm is BROKEN (kernel crash at step 1) - relevant to the portable Linux build.

QUANT MATRIX VERIFIED LIVE (HF API, Comfy-Org/Krea-2) - user was HALF RIGHT about mxfp8 and the wrong half matters. TURBO has FIVE transformer variants: bf16 24.48GB / fp8_scaled 12.24GB (WHAT WE SHIP) / int8_convrot 12.57GB / mxfp8 12.60GB / nvfp4 7.15GB. RAW has only THREE: bf16 / fp8_scaled / int8_convrot. *** RAW HAS NO mxfp8 AND NO nvfp4 *** (user said `both for turbo and the full model` - not true for mxfp8/nvfp4). CONSEQUENCE: a variants.arch axis on RAW cannot offer a Blackwell-native option; Raws Blackwell users fall back to fp8_scaled. GATE 2 CLEARED: all three quants are NATIVE in ComfyUI 0.27 - comfy/quant_ops.py QUANT_ALGOS registers mxfp8, nvfp4, int8_tensorwise. NO community fork needed => the disqualifier that killed GGUF (MPI-190) does NOT apply here. GATE 1 CLEARED: weights confirmed to exist w/ sizes (hashes to capture at upload). So the arch axis for TURBO is a genuine THREE-way: blackwell=>mxfp8, modern(Ada/Ampere)=>int8_convrot, legacy=>fp8_scaled fallback. Near-copy of LTXs variants.arch but WITHOUT workflowSuffix (Krea2 graph is identical across quants - only the UNETLoader weight differs; LTX needs _mxfp8/_fp8 workflow files because generate_ltx.py emits them). NEW FINDING: nvfp4 at 7.15GB is the ONLY variant that fits 8GB VRAM RESIDENT - but it is Blackwell-only, and Blackwell cards mostly have >=16GB, so it solves a VRAM problem for GPUs that dont have one. LIKELY LOW VALUE; added as gate 7, confirm before spending an upload. FIVE GATES STILL OPEN: reproduce the 1.92x on OUR 4060 Ti (Ada, `modern`) since the bench is a 3090 (Ampere) and Adas INT8 path differs - DO NOT ASSUME IT TRANSFERS; seeded face A/B at 1K+2K; Blackwell A/B is now mxfp8-vs-fp8 (NOT int8) for Turbo; per-variant R2 upload + ~12.6GB per-user download cost (archVariantOptions makes it an install TOGGLE per MPI-209, not a forced download); nvfp4 value. Doc updated + index row rewritten. Krea2 SHIPS fp8_scaled ONLY; quant work = separate card.
